quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 3 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: Identifying Angle Pairs
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 3.1 Lines and Angles Formed by a Transversal

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines. Which pair of angles lie on the same side of the transversal and in corresponding positions relative to the two lines?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles occupy the same relative position at each intersection. For example, the upper-left angle at one intersection and the upper-left angle at the other are corresponding angles.

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines. Which pair of angles lie between the two lines and on opposite sides of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles lie between the two lines (interior) and on opposite sides of the transversal (alternate). They form a Z-shaped pattern.

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines. Which pair of angles lie between the two lines and on the same side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles (also called same-side interior angles) lie between the two lines and on the same side of the transversal. They form a C-shaped pattern.

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines. Which pair of angles lie outside the two lines and on opposite sides of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Alternate exterior angles lie outside the two lines (exterior) and on opposite sides of the transversal (alternate). They are congruent when the lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, $\angle 3$ and $\angle 6$ are inside the two lines and on opposite sides of the transversal. What type of angle pair are they?
questions.explanation: Angles that are inside (between) the two lines and on opposite sides of the transversal are alternate interior angles.

questions.question: In the figure, $\angle 1$ and $\angle 5$ are in the same position at each intersection. What type of angle pair are they?
questions.explanation: Angles that occupy the same relative position at each intersection of the transversal with the two lines are corresponding angles.

questions.question: In the figure, $\angle 4$ and $\angle 6$ are between the two lines and on the same side of the transversal. What type of angle pair are they?
questions.explanation: Angles that are between the two lines and on the same side of the transversal are consecutive interior angles.

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines, forming eight angles. How many pairs of corresponding angles are there?
questions.explanation: With eight angles formed by a transversal intersecting two lines, there are four pairs of corresponding angles — one pair for each position (upper-left, upper-right, lower-left, lower-right).

questions.question: A transversal intersects two lines, forming eight angles. How many pairs of alternate interior angles are there?
questions.explanation: There are two pairs of alternate interior angles when a transversal intersects two lines. Each pair consists of angles on opposite sides of the transversal, both between the two lines.

questions.question: Which statement correctly distinguishes alternate interior angles from consecutive interior angles?
options: Alternate interior angles are on opposite sides of the transversal; consecutive interior angles are on the same side
Alternate interior angles are outside the two lines; consecutive interior angles are inside
Alternate interior angles are at different intersections; consecutive interior angles share an intersection
Alternate interior angles form a linear pair; consecutive interior angles are vertical angles
questions.explanation: Both angle pairs lie between the two lines. The key difference is that alternate interior angles are on opposite sides of the transversal, while consecutive interior angles are on the same side.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can confidently identify all angle pairs formed by a transversal intersecting two lines.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main angle pairs but review the distinction between alternate interior and consecutive interior angles.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: corresponding (same position), alternate interior (inside, opposite sides), alternate exterior (outside, opposite sides), and consecutive interior (inside, same side).
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A transversal creates four angle pair types: corresponding, alternate interior, alternate exterior, and consecutive interior. Learn the location pattern of each.
